ignored_examples.snippet: Subject: Re: Sharing something that might help!\n\nBody: I’m no longer at work with Zoca and I’m still waiting You guys take me out of the system. On Sun, Feb 1, 2026 at 5:42 PM Mannica at Zoca < success@zoca.com > wrote: Hi beauty&soul Hap

cause_analysis.narrative: Beauty & Soul Spa churned on 2026-02-02 after 163 days with Zoca. Sentiment turned negative on 2025-12-30 (app_chat) — first complaint keyword: "problem". Classification: results/service failure — the client raised substantive complaints that were either unanswered or unresolved. 1 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days — this is the clearest operational failure on Zoca's side.
recommendations: [Product] Customer raised results concerns (leads/bookings/ROI) — this is the highest-ROI churn to prevent. Establish a weekly 'performance check-in' for any customer whose core metric drops 20% vs prior 4 weeks.
[Escalation] Results complaints should auto-escalate to SP + AM within 24h with a written action plan shared back to the client.
[SLA] 1 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days. Enforce a hard rule: every inbound gets a human response within 24h, every day of the week.

[SLA example] On 2026-02-02 via email, client wrote: "Subject: Re: Sharing something that might help!\n\nBody: I’m no longer at work with Zoca and I’m still waiting You guys …" — no outbound response in 30 days.

However, I’ve also dropped you a text on the Zoca app.    I sincerely apologize for your experience and would really appreciate the opportunity to take your honest feedback and see if there’s anything I can fix immediately, witho…

Hi Helzo - I sincerely apologize. You're absolutely right, and I'm taking care of this immediately. I've escalated your cancellation request to my Client Experience Head and will surely get back to you - the first thing tomorrow’s morning.
Did you get a chance to go through the performance report?

Meanwhile, I'd love to understand how I can help you in a better way. Also, if you could just review the report, it's entirely your decision whether you want to stay or cancel. But viewing the report will definitely give you insights that could help you evaluate any company you're considering working with.
